history.qwen_observation: Compilation failed in upgrade_spring_boot_2_7 because reactor-core upgraded and moved reactor.retry.Retry to reactor.util.retry.Retry, causing a type mismatch.
history.qwen_rationale: Insert a ChangePackage step before the Spring Boot 2.7 upgrade to migrate the deprecated reactor.retry.Retry import to reactor.util.retry.Retry, aligning the source code with the reactor-core version pulled in by Spring Boot 2.7.

history.qwen_observation: The fix_reactor_retry_import step failed because reactor.util.retry does not exist in the Reactor version bundled with Spring Boot 2.1.6; the package was only introduced in Reactor 3.4+ (Spring Boot 2.4+).
history.qwen_rationale: Moving the ChangePackage step to execute after upgrade_spring_boot_2_7 ensures the upgraded Reactor dependency (which contains reactor.util.retry) is on the classpath before the source migration runs, allowing compilation to succeed.

history.qwen_observation: The `upgrade_spring_boot_2_7` step fails compilation because `reactor-core` upgraded by Spring Boot 2.7 moved `Retry` from `reactor.retry` to `reactor.util.retry`, but the source code still imports the old package.
history.qwen_rationale: By combining the Spring Boot 2.7 upgrade and the `ChangePackage` migration into a single step, both the dependency upgrade and the source import rewrite execute before the post-step `mvn compile` check. This ensures the new `reactor.util.retry` package is on the classpath when the compiler validates the rewritten imports.

history.qwen_observation: Compilation failed during Spring Boot 2.7 upgrade due to a manual ChangePackage recipe causing an API mismatch with reactor.util.retry.Retry, and Lombok annotation processor failing to generate getters and log fields after compiler plugin updates.
history.qwen_rationale: Removed the manual ChangePackage recipe from the Spring Boot 2.7 step to allow the built-in Reactor migration to handle the Retry API changes correctly. Added a pom_patch step to explicitly place Lombok on the annotation processor path, ensuring annotation processing succeeds after the maven-compiler-plugin configuration changes.
